title: Banish Chills with These 11 Amazing Herbal Essential Oils

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for chills because it helps to open up airways and increase blood flow to the skin, which can help to warm the body and relieve feelings of coldness.
Quercetin is good for chills because it helps to reduce inflammation and calm the body, thus relieving the shivering and discomfort caused by the cold.
Catechin is good for chills because it helps to fight off the infection that causes the chills and also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce fever and body aches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is often used to help with chills because of its natural decongestant properties. When you have a chill, your body may feel cold and your airways can become blocked with mucus. The oil's main ingredient, eucalyptol, helps to break down this mucus and makes it easier to breathe. It also has an anti-inflammatory effect, which can reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to take deep breaths. When inhaled, the oil can also help to clear your mind and reduce stress, which can often accompany chills. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has a cooling sensation when applied topically, which can help to bring down a fever and make you feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, you'll need fresh lemongrass leaves, a clean glass jar, a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a small saucepan. Start by washing the lemongrass leaves thoroughly with water, then pat them dry with a paper towel. Next, tie the lemongrass leaves into small bunches and place them in the glass jar. Pour the carrier oil over the lemongrass leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Now, place the jar in the saucepan and add water to about an inch below the jar's bottom. Heat the water over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the lemongrass leaves have released their oil. Remove the jar from the saucepan and let it cool. Strain the oil through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the lemongrass oil in a cool, dark place. Use this oil in a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for a massage to help relieve chills and other cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for chills because it helps to reduce inflammation and ease body pain, making it easier to get rid of the chills and feel warm again.
Shogaol is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and ease cold-related discomfort.
Zingiberene helps to reduce chills because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that calm the body and relieve fever-related discomfort.
Curcumenol helps to reduce chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the body and relieve the discomfort caused by the cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as Ginger oil, is a natural remedy for chills. It works by warming up the body and helping to reduce inflammation. When you have chills, your body is trying to fight off a cold or flu virus. Ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm down the immune system and reduce the fever. It also has a warming effect that helps to increase blood circulation and heat up the body. This helps to relieve the chills and make you feel more comfortable. Additionally, Ginger oil has antiviral properties that help to fight off the virus and prevent it from spreading. When inhaled, Ginger oil can help to relieve congestion and coughs that often come with chills. It can be used in a diffuser, added to a warm bath, or applied topically to the skin with a carrier oil to help relieve chills and other cold symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils, also known as lemongrass oil, which helps with chills, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need a large quantity of fresh lemongrass stalks, which you can find in most health food stores or grow yourself. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a sharp knife for cutting the lemongrass, and a steam distillation equipment or a home distillation kit, which can be purchased online or at a local herbal store. To begin, cut the lemongrass into small pieces and place them in the distillation equipment, making sure to follow the manufacturer's instructions. Next, heat the equipment to create steam, which will pass through the lemongrass and release its essential oils. The steam will then be cooled, and the essential oil will separate from the water, which you can then collect and store in a glass bottle. It's essential to note that the process of steam distillation requires caution and safety measures, so make sure to follow proper safety guidelines and consult with an expert if you're new to herbal distillation. Once you have the lemongrass oil, you can use it in aromatherapy by adding a few drops to a diffuser or mixing it with a carrier oil for topical use, helping to relieve chills and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and soothe the body, thereby providing relief from the discomfort associated with chills.
Carnosic acid is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and soothe the nervous system.
Cineole is good for chills because it has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve cold and flu symptoms, open airways, and ease body aches associated with chills.
Bornyl acetate is good for chills because it has natural warming and decongestant properties that help to reduce body temperature and relieve cold symptoms.
Oleanolic acid is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary essential oil, is a popular natural remedy to help with chills. This oil is known for its warming properties that help increase blood circulation and relieve coldness. When inhaled, rosemary essential oil can help open up airways and promote a sense of warmth and comfort. Its antimicrobial properties can also help combat underlying infections that may be causing the chills. Additionally, rosemary essential oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce body temperature and alleviate discomfort. Many people also find the scent of rosemary to be invigorating and refreshing, which can help boost their mood and reduce feelings of fatigue and weakness associated with chills.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for relieving chills, start by harvesting fresh leaves from the Lemongrass plant, preferably in the early morning when the oil content is highest. Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent any contamination. Next, fill a clean glass container with the dried Lemongrass leaves, and pour a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil over them. The ratio of Lemongrass leaves to carrier oil should be about one part leaves to three parts oil. Seal the container and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the container every few days to ensure even infusion. After the infusion process is complete, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the Lemongrass essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. This homemade Lemongrass essential oil can be used topically to help alleviate chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for chills

Linalool is good for chills because it helps to relax the body and reduce muscle tension, which can help to warm the body and relieve cold feelings.
Limonene is good for chills because it has natural decongestant properties that help to relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and feel warmer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oil, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, has natural properties that help alleviate chills. It contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. When inhaled, Ceylon cinnamon oil can help relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, reducing shivering and chills. Its warm, spicy aroma can also help increase blood circulation and soothe the body. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off underlying infections that may be causing the chills. Applying it topically, mixed with a carrier oil, can also provide local relief from cold and flu symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, you will need fresh lemongrass leaves and a distillation setup. First, harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and wash them thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, tie the lemongrass leaves in small bunches and place them in a distillation apparatus, making sure the leaves are not too crowded. Then, heat the apparatus using steam, allowing the lemongrass essential oil to vaporize and rise into a condenser coil where it cools down and turns back into a liquid. Collect the resulting essential oil in a container, making sure to store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency. Once the oil is ready, you can use it to help alleviate chills by applying a few drops to your skin, inhaling its vapors, or mixing it with a carrier oil for a massage. Remember to use caution when handling essential oils, as they can be potent and irritate skin if not used properly.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms.
Quercetin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and relieve shivering, making it a natural remedy to soothe cold symptoms.
Polyphenols are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and ease discomfort caused by fever.
Flavonoids are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and relieve fever, which can cause chills.
Iridoid glycosides are good for chills because they have been shown to help reduce fever and relieve body aches by acting as a natural antipyretic agent.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oil is known for its ability to help with chills. The oil is made from the flowers and roots of the Echinacea plant, which is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to boost the immune system. When you have chills, it's often a sign that your body is fighting off an infection. Echinacea purpurea oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the body. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the infection and speed up the healing process. When inhaled, the oil can help open up airways and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe. The oil can also be applied topically to help reduce fever and ease body aches. By using Echinacea purpurea essential oil, you may be able to help your body recover from illness faster and feel better sooner.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, start by gathering fresh or dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves. You'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a dark glass bottle for storing the final product. Begin by washing the leaves thoroughly and drying them with a clean towel. Next, place the dried leaves in the glass jar and pour coconut or jojoba oil over them, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the leaves. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, and discard the leaves. You'll now have a natural, anti-inflammatory essential oil that can be used in aromatherapy to help relieve chills and colds. Always dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, and use it in moderation.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for chills because it has a warming effect on the body, helping to increase circulation and reduce the feeling of coldness.

Linalool is good for chills because it acts as a natural relaxant, reducing stress and anxiety that can cause the body to feel cold.
Caryophyllene is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body heat loss and relieve cold or flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oil, also known as thyme oil, has been used to help relieve chills for a long time. The oil has antibacterial properties that can help fight off infections that may be causing the chills. This means it can help reduce the body temperature and make you feel better faster. Thyme oil also has a warm and comforting effect on the body, which can help alleviate feelings of coldness and shivers. Additionally, the oil has expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and coughs that often come with chills. When applied topically, thyme oil can help increase circulation, which can also help warm the body and reduce chills.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, start by harvesting fresh lemongrass leaves and stems from the Cymbopogon citratus plant. Next, clean and dry the lemongrass thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage. Then, steam distill the lemongrass to extract the essential oil. This process involves heating water in a distillation apparatus and then passing the steam through the lemongrass, causing the oil to be released and collected in a separate container. The resulting essential oil can be filtered and bottled for use. Alternatively, you can also use a cold-pressing method, but this may not be as effective for extracting the oil from the lemongrass. Once you have the essential oil, you can use it in a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for topical use to help alleviate chills and other symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for chills because it helps to relax the body, reduce fever, and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and feel better when you're cold.
Linalool is good for chills because it helps the body relax and reduce stress, which can make the chills go away and help you feel warm and calm again.
Catechin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and relieve body aches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for years to help with chills. This is because it has antimicrobial properties, which means it can help fight off infections that can cause chills. When applied to the skin, tea tree oil can help reduce inflammation and ease the discomfort associated with chills. Its decongestant properties can also help relieve congestion and runny nose, which are common symptoms of the flu or colds that can cause chills. Tea tree oil can be added to a warm bath or applied to the body with a carrier oil to help soothe and calm the body. Its antiviral properties can also help combat viral infections that can cause chills, such as the flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemon grass, herbal essential oils that help with chills, you will need some fresh lemon grass leaves, a large bowl, a steamer or a pot, a glass jar with a lid, and a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil. First, cut about a pound of fresh lemon grass leaves into small pieces and place them in the steamer or pot. Add enough water to the steamer or pot to prevent the lemon grass from burning. Next, steam the lemon grass for about an hour to release its oils and flavor. After steaming, let the lemon grass cool down and then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl. Discard the solids and add a carrier oil to the lemon grass extract in a 1:3 ratio, meaning for one part of lemon grass extract, add three parts of carrier oil. Mix well and transfer the mixture to a glass jar with a lid. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for a few days to allow the mixture to mature. After a few days, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve again and transfer the herbal essential oil to a smaller glass bottle with a dropper lid. Your Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil is now ready to use, and you can apply a few drops to your skin or inhale it to help with chills.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce body temperature and soothe the body.

Flavonoids are good for chills because they help the body to relax and reduce inflammation, which can cause chills by lowering the body temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil, also known as licorice root oil, is known to help with chills due to its warming properties. When applied topically, it can increase blood flow to the skin and muscles, helping to relax and warm up the body. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience chills due to cold temperatures or poor circulation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, licorice root oil has been traditionally used to support immune function and help the body fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to chills. By promoting a sense of warmth and comfort, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil can help to alleviate chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with chills, first, you'll need some fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, also known as lemongrass. Harvest the leaves from the plant when they are young and tender, and make sure they are free of any dirt or debris. Next, wash the leaves with clean water to remove any impurities. Then, chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a glass jar. Pour a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. This process is called infusing, and it allows the active compounds from the lemongrass leaves to transfer into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to help reduce chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for chills

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and ease body aches, making it a natural remedy for relieving cold and flu symptoms.
Kaempferol is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce body temperature and soothe the muscles.
Isorhapontigenin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and relieve discomfort caused by chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is a natural remedy that can help with chills. The oil is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and ease body aches that come with chills. Ginkgo biloba essential oil can also improve circulation, which helps to bring warmth to the body and reduce shivering. Additionally, the oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of chills. When used in aromatherapy or applied topically, Ginkgo biloba essential oil can help to calm the body and mind, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress, which can also contribute to chills. The oil's ability to improve respiratory function can also help to ease congestion and coughs that often come with chills.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, you will need a few basic materials. First, you will need fresh lemongrass leaves, which are rich in citral, a natural compound that helps to reduce chills and body aches. Next, you will need a clean glass jar, a sharp knife, a mortar and pestle, and a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Start by washing the lemongrass leaves in cold water, and then pat them dry with a clean towel. Using your sharp knife, finely chop the lemongrass leaves, and then use your mortar and pestle to crush them into a pulp. Transfer the crushed lemongrass to your glass jar, and pour in your carrier oil, making sure that the lemongrass is completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the lemongrass. After the extraction process is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. Your Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil is now ready to use, and can be applied topically to help reduce chills and body aches.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, also known as lavender oil, is often used to help with chills. This is because it has natural properties that can help reduce inflammation and relax the body. When you have chills, your body is fighting off an infection, and your muscles can become tense. Lavender oil can help calm your muscles and reduce stress, making you feel more relaxed and comfortable. It also has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help fight off the infection that's causing your chills. Additionally, lavender oil has a soothing scent that can help calm your mind and body, reducing your body temperature and making you feel better. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with chills, first you need to obtain the fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as lemongrass. You can buy these from a local herb store or grow them in your garden. Next, you need to steam distill the leaves to extract the essential oil. This process involves placing the lemongrass leaves in a distillation apparatus and heating them with steam, which releases the oil from the leaves. The steam and oil mixture is then cooled and the oil is separated from the water. You need to use a good quality distillation equipment and follow proper safety guidelines to avoid any accidents. After distillation, filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle to preserve its quality. To use the oil, simply add a few drops to a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for topical application. The Cymbopogon citratus essential oil has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce chills and fever.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Limonene is good for chills because it has natural decongestant properties that help relieve cold and flu symptoms by loosening mucus and opening up airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing body temperature.
Linalool is good for chills because it helps reduce body temperature by increasing blood flow and relaxing muscles, making it a natural remedy for people who feel cold or shivery.
Citral is good for chills because it has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to warm the body by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can make you feel cold.
Geranial helps to reduce chills because it has natural decongestant properties that help to break down mucus and open airways, making it easier to breathe and feel warm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oil, also known as lemon grass oil, is often used to help with chills due to its warming properties. It contains compounds like citral and geraniol that have a natural ability to raise the body temperature. When applied topically or inhaled, these compounds can stimulate the body's natural warming response, helping to reduce the feeling of coldness and chills. Additionally, lemon grass oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu, such as soreness and discomfort. Its antimicrobial properties can also help to fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the chills. By promoting a sense of warmth and comfort, Cymbopogon citratus essential oil can provide relief from chills and help to improve overall well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with chills, first you need to harvest fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers. Choose leaves with vibrant green color and no signs of wilting or damage. Harvest them in the early morning or late afternoon when the temperature is cooler. Next, gently rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water to remove dirt and impurities. Then, dry the leaves and flowers using a clean towel or by placing them in a warm, dry, and well-ventilated area. Once they are completely dry, place them in a clean glass container and store them in a cool, dark place for at least 2-3 weeks to allow the essential oils to mature and develop their flavor and aroma. After the drying period, use a steam distillation process to extract the essential oils from the dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers. This process involves heating water to create steam, which then passes through the dried plant material, causing the essential oils to be released into a separate container. Collect the extracted essential oil and store it in a clean glass bottle, sealed tightly to preserve its quality and shelf life.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for chills

intro_desc: When you have chills, your body is trying to fight off an infection or illness. Using herbal essential oils can help relieve these symptoms and make you feel better. Two effective oils for chills are Eucalyptus globulus and Zingiber officinale. Eucalyptus globulus is good because it has decongestant properties that can help clear mucus from your airways, making it easier to breathe. This can help reduce fever and chills. Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and ease body aches, which often accompany chills. When you're feeling cold and miserable, these oils can help ease your symptoms and make you feel more comfortable. This is important because when you're not feeling well, it's hard to do everyday tasks, take care of yourself, and enjoy life. By using herbal essential oils to relieve chills, you can get back to your normal routine and enjoy activities and time with loved ones.
supplementary_best: A combination of Eucalyptus globulus and Cinnamomum verum essential oils can help remedy chills. Eucalyptus globulus oil has decongestant properties that help relieve cold and flu symptoms by thinning mucus in the respiratory tract. Its cooling effect can also help reduce fever and alleviate chills. Cinnamomum verum oil, on the other hand, has warming properties that can help increase blood circulation and warm the body from the inside out. This helps to counteract the cold and clammy feeling associated with chills. When blended together, these two oils create a balanced effect that can help alleviate chills and promote a feeling of warmth and comfort.
supplementary_avoid: If you have chills, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid. One of these oils is 'Hyssopus officinalis' (Hyssop), which can be quite strong and may cause more discomfort. Another oil to be cautious with is 'Piper nigrum' (Black Pepper), as its stimulating properties could increase your body temperature and make you feel even chillier. Also, 'Sassafras albidum' (Sassafras) oil may not be suitable, as it can cause stomach upset and other issues that might make your chills worse. 'Silybum marianum' (Milk Thistle) oil is another one to avoid, as it can have a drying effect on the body, which might make your chills more intense. Lastly, 'Teucrium chamaedrys' (Wood Sage) oil may not be the best choice, as its bitter properties could cause digestive issues that might exacerbate your chills.
supplementary_other_remedies: When you have chills, your body is trying to fight off an infection. Besides using herbal essential oils, there are other natural remedies that can help. Drinking warm tea, like ginger or peppermint, can help raise your body temperature and make you feel better. These teas have properties that help soothe your stomach and calm your body. Chicken soup has also been shown to be effective in helping with chills, as it contains a protein called cysteine that has been known to thin mucus and make it easier to cough up. This can help alleviate congestion and make you feel more comfortable. Taking a warm bath with Epsom salt can also help, as the magnesium in the salt can help relax your muscles and reduce inflammation. Additionally, some people find that using a humidifier can help with chills, as it adds moisture to the air and helps to loosen up mucus, making it easier to breathe.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have chills, they are often experiencing a combination of symptoms. One common issue is a sore throat. Many medicinal herbs can help soothe a sore throat, such as slippery elm, which forms a protective barrier on the throat and reduces inflammation.  Some people with chills may also have a fever, which is usually a sign that the body is fighting an infection. Echinacea is a popular medicinal herb that helps boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can help bring down a fever.  Another issue that people with chills may experience is a cough, which can be irritated by the cold air. Thyme is an herb that has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.  In addition to these symptoms, people with chills may also experience fatigue and body aches, which can be relieved with the help of herbs like ginger and willow bark. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort, while willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that can help reduce pain and fever.  Lastly, people with chills may experience digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, which can be helped with the use of herbs like peppermint and licorice root. Peppermint can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea, while licorice root can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
